verses.bsb: “Phinehas son of Eleazar, the son of Aaron the priest, has turned My wrath away from the Israelites; for he was zealous for My sake among them, so that I did not consume the Israelites in My zeal.

verses.kjv: Wherefore say, Behold, I give unto him my covenant of peace:
verses.bsb: Declare, therefore, that I am granting him My covenant of peace.

verses.kjv: And he shall have it, and his seed after him, even the covenant of an everlasting priesthood; because he was zealous for his God, and made an atonement for the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: It will be a covenant of permanent priesthood for him and his descendants, because he was zealous for his God and made atonement for the Israelites.”

verses.kjv: Now the name of the Israelite that was slain, even that was slain with the Midianitish woman, was Zimri, the son of Salu, a prince of a chief house among the Simeonites.
verses.bsb: The name of the Israelite who was slain with the Midianite woman was Zimri son of Salu, the leader of a Simeonite family.

verses.kjv: And the name of the Midianitish woman that was slain was Cozbi, the daughter of Zur; he was head over a people, and of a chief house in Midian.
verses.bsb: And the name of the slain Midianite woman was Cozbi, the daughter of Zur, a tribal chief of a Midianite family.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: Vex the Midianites, and smite them:
verses.bsb: “Attack the Midianites and strike them dead.

verses.kjv: For they vex you with their wiles, wherewith they have beguiled you in the matter of Peor, and in the matter of Cozbi, the daughter of a prince of Midian, their sister, which was slain in the day of the plague for Peor’s sake.
verses.bsb: For they assailed you deceitfully when they seduced you in the matter of Peor and their sister Cozbi, the daughter of the Midianite leader, the woman who was killed on the day the plague came because of Peor.”

verses.kjv: AND it came to pass after the plague, that the Lord spake unto Moses and unto Eleazar the son of Aaron the priest, saying,
verses.bsb: After the plague had ended, the LORD said to Moses and Eleazar son of Aaron the priest,

verses.kjv: Take the sum of all the congregation of the children of Israel, from twenty years old and upward, throughout their fathers’ house, all that are able to go to war in Israel.
verses.bsb: “Take a census of the whole congregation of Israel by the houses of their fathers—all those twenty years of age or older who can serve in the army of Israel.”

verses.kjv: And Moses and Eleazar the priest spake with them in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho, saying,
verses.bsb: So on the plains of Moab by the Jordan, across from Jericho, Moses and Eleazar the priest issued the instruction,

verses.kjv: Take the sum of the people, from twenty years old and upward; as the Lord commanded Moses and the children of Israel, which went forth out of the land of Egypt.
verses.bsb: “Take a census of the men twenty years of age or older, as the LORD has commanded Moses.”  And these were the Israelites who came out of the land of Egypt:

verses.kjv: ¶ Reuben, the eldest son of Israel: the children of Reuben; Hanoch, of whom cometh the family of the Hanochites: of Pallu, the family of the Palluites:
verses.bsb: Reuben was the firstborn of Israel. These were the descendants of Reuben:  The Hanochite clan from Hanoch,   the Palluite clan from Pallu,

verses.kjv: Of Hezron, the family of the Hezronites: of Carmi, the family of the Carmites.
verses.bsb: the Hezronite clan from Hezron,   and the Carmite clan from Carmi.

verses.kjv: These are the families of the Reubenites: and they that were numbered of them were forty and three thousand and seven hundred and thirty.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Reuben, and their registration numbered 43,730.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Pallu; Eliab.
verses.bsb: Now the son of Pallu was Eliab,

verses.kjv: And the sons of Eliab; Nemuel, and Dathan, and Abiram. This is that Dathan and Abiram, which were famous in the congregation, who strove against Moses and against Aaron in the company of Korah, when they strove against the Lord:
verses.bsb: and the sons of Eliab were Nemuel, Dathan, and Abiram.  It was Dathan and Abiram, chosen by the congregation, who fought against Moses and Aaron with the followers of Korah who rebelled against the LORD.

verses.kjv: And the earth opened her mouth, and swallowed them up together with Korah, when that company died, what time the fire devoured two hundred and fifty men: and they became a sign.
verses.bsb: And the earth opened its mouth and swallowed them along with Korah, whose followers died when the fire consumed 250 men. They serve as a warning sign.

verses.kjv: Notwithstanding the children of Korah died not.
verses.bsb: However, the line of Korah did not die out.

verses.kjv: ¶ The sons of Simeon after their families: of Nemuel, the family of the Nemuelites: of Jamin, the family of the Jaminites: of Jachin, the family of the Jachinites:
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Simeon by their clans:  The Nemuelite clan from Nemuel,   the Jaminite clan from Jamin,   the Jachinite clan from Jachin,

verses.kjv: Of Zerah, the family of the Zarhites: of Shaul, the family of the Shaulites.
verses.bsb: the Zerahite clan from Zerah,   and the Shaulite clan from Shaul.

verses.kjv: These are the families of the Simeonites, twenty and two thousand and two hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Simeon, and there were 22,200 men.

verses.kjv: ¶ The children of Gad after their families: of Zephon, the family of the Zephonites: of Haggi, the family of the Haggites: of Shuni, the family of the Shunites:
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Gad by their clans:  The Zephonite clan from Zephon,   the Haggite clan from Haggi,   the Shunite clan from Shuni,

verses.kjv: Of Ozni, the family of the Oznites: of Eri, the family of the Erites:
verses.bsb: the Oznite clan from Ozni,   the Erite clan from Eri,

verses.kjv: Of Arod, the family of the Arodites: of Areli, the family of the Arelites.
verses.bsb: the Arodite clan from Arod,   and the Arelite clan from Areli.

verses.kjv: These are the families of the children of Gad according to those that were numbered of them, forty thousand and five hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Gad, and their registration numbered 40,500.

verses.kjv: ¶ The sons of Judah were Er and Onan: and Er and Onan died in the land of Canaan.
verses.bsb: The sons of Judah were Er and Onan, but they died in the land of Canaan.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Judah after their families were; of Shelah, the family of the Shelanites: of Pharez, the family of the Pharzites: of Zerah, the family of the Zarhites.
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Judah by their clans:  The Shelanite clan from Shelah,   the Perezite clan from Perez,   and the Zerahite clan from Zerah.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Pharez were; of Hezron, the family of the Hezronites: of Hamul, the family of the Hamulites.
verses.bsb: And these were the descendants of Perez:   the Hezronite clan from Hezron   and the Hamulite clan from Hamul.

verses.kjv: These are the families of Judah according to those that were numbered of them, threescore and sixteen thousand and five hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Judah, and their registration numbered 76,500.

verses.kjv: ¶ Of the sons of Issachar after their families: of Tola, the family of the Tolaites: of Pua, the family of the Punites:
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Issachar by their clans:  The Tolaite clan from Tola,   the Punite clan from Puvah,

verses.kjv: Of Jashub, the family of the Jashubites: of Shimron, the family of the Shimronites.
verses.bsb: the Jashubite clan from Jashub,   and the Shimronite clan from Shimron.

verses.kjv: These are the families of Issachar according to those that were numbered of them, threescore and four thousand and three hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Issachar, and their registration numbered 64,300.

verses.kjv: ¶ Of the sons of Zebulun after their families: of Sered, the family of the Sardites: of Elon, the family of the Elonites: of Jahleel, the family of the Jahleelites.
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Zebulun by their clans:  The Seredite clan from Sered,   the Elonite clan from Elon,   and the Jahleelite clan from Jahleel.

verses.kjv: These are the families of the Zebulunites according to those that were numbered of them, threescore thousand and five hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Zebulun, and their registration numbered 60,500.

verses.kjv: ¶ The sons of Joseph after their families were Manasseh and Ephraim.
verses.bsb: The descendants of Joseph included the clans of Manasseh and Ephraim.

verses.kjv: Of the sons of Manasseh: of Machir, the family of the Machirites: and Machir begat Gilead: of Gilead come the family of the Gileadites.
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Manasseh:  The Machirite clan from Machir, the father of Gilead,   and the Gileadite clan from Gilead.

verses.kjv: These are the sons of Gilead: of Jeezer, the family of the Jeezerites: of Helek, the family of the Helekites:
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Gilead:   the Iezerite clan from Iezer,   the Helekite clan from Helek,

verses.kjv: And of Asriel, the family of the Asrielites: and of Shechem, the family of the Shechemites:
verses.bsb: the Asrielite clan from Asriel,   the Shechemite clan from Shechem,

verses.kjv: And of Shemida, the family of the Shemidaites: and of Hepher, the family of the Hepherites.
verses.bsb: the Shemidaite clan from Shemida,   and the Hepherite clan from Hepher.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Zelophehad the son of Hepher had no sons, but daughters: and the names of the daughters of Zelophehad were Mahlah, and Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.
verses.bsb: Now Zelophehad son of Hepher had no sons but only daughters. The names of his daughters were Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah.

verses.kjv: These are the families of Manasseh, and those that were numbered of them, fifty and two thousand and seven hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Manasseh, and their registration numbered 52,700.

verses.kjv: ¶ These are the sons of Ephraim after their families: of Shuthelah, the family of the Shuthalhites: of Becher, the family of the Bachrites: of Tahan, the family of the Tahanites.
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Ephraim by their clans:  The Shuthelahite clan from Shuthelah,   the Becherite clan from Becher,   and the Tahanite clan from Tahan.

verses.kjv: And these are the sons of Shuthelah: of Eran, the family of the Eranites.
verses.bsb: And the descendants of Shuthelah were the Eranite clan from Eran.

verses.kjv: These are the families of the sons of Ephraim according to those that were numbered of them, thirty and two thousand and five hundred. These are the sons of Joseph after their families.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Ephraim, and their registration numbered 32,500.  These clans were the descendants of Joseph.

verses.kjv: ¶ The sons of Benjamin after their families: of Bela, the family of the Belaites: of Ashbel, the family of the Ashbelites: of Ahiram, the family of the Ahiramites:
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Benjamin by their clans:  The Belaite clan from Bela,   the Ashbelite clan from Ashbel,   the Ahiramite clan from Ahiram,

verses.kjv: Of Shupham, the family of the Shuphamites: of Hupham, the family of the Huphamites.
verses.bsb: the Shuphamite clan from Shupham,   and the Huphamite clan from Hupham.

verses.kjv: And the sons of Bela were Ard and Naaman: of Ard, the family of the Ardites: and of Naaman, the family of the Naamites.
verses.bsb: And the descendants of Bela from Ard and Naaman were the Ardite clan from Ard and the Naamite clan from Naaman.

verses.kjv: These are the sons of Benjamin after their families: and they that were numbered of them were forty and five thousand and six hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Benjamin, and their registration numbered 45,600.

verses.kjv: ¶ These are the sons of Dan after their families: of Shuham, the family of the Shuhamites. These are the families of Dan after their families.
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Dan by their clans:  The Shuhamite clan from Shuham.   These were the clans of Dan.

verses.kjv: All the families of the Shuhamites, according to those that were numbered of them, were threescore and four thousand and four hundred.
verses.bsb: All of them were Shuhamite clans, and their registration numbered 64,400.

verses.kjv: ¶ Of the children of Asher after their families: of Jimna, the family of the Jimnites: of Jesui, the family of the Jesuites: of Beriah, the family of the Beriites.
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Asher by their clans:  The Imnite clan from Imnah,   the Ishvite clan from Ishvi,   and the Beriite clan from Beriah.

verses.kjv: Of the sons of Beriah: of Heber, the family of the Heberites: of Malchiel, the family of the Malchielites.
verses.bsb: And these were the descendants of Beriah:   the Heberite clan from Heber   and the Malchielite clan from Malchiel.

verses.kjv: And the name of the daughter of Asher was Sarah.
verses.bsb: And the name of Asher’s daughter was Serah.

verses.kjv: These are the families of the sons of Asher according to those that were numbered of them; who were fifty and three thousand and four hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Asher, and their registration numbered 53,400.

verses.kjv: ¶ Of the sons of Naphtali after their families: of Jahzeel, the family of the Jahzeelites: of Guni, the family of the Gunites:
verses.bsb: These were the descendants of Naphtali by their clans:  The Jahzeelite clan from Jahzeel,   the Gunite clan from Guni,

verses.kjv: Of Jezer, the family of the Jezerites: of Shillem, the family of the Shillemites.
verses.bsb: the Jezerite clan from Jezer,   and the Shillemite clan from Shillem.

verses.kjv: These are the families of Naphtali according to their families: and they that were numbered of them were forty and five thousand and four hundred.
verses.bsb: These were the clans of Naphtali, and their registration numbered 45,400.

verses.kjv: These were the numbered of the children of Israel, six hundred thousand and a thousand seven hundred and thirty.
verses.bsb: These men of Israel numbered 601,730 in all.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: Unto these the land shall be divided for an inheritance according to the number of names.
verses.bsb: “The land is to be divided among the tribes as an inheritance, according to the number of names.

verses.kjv: To many thou shalt give the more inheritance, and to few thou shalt give the less inheritance: to every one shall his inheritance be given according to those that were numbered of him.
verses.bsb: Increase the inheritance for a large tribe and decrease it for a small one; each tribe is to receive its inheritance according to the number of those registered.

verses.kjv: Notwithstanding the land shall be divided by lot: according to the names of the tribes of their fathers they shall inherit.
verses.bsb: Indeed, the land must be divided by lot; they shall receive their inheritance according to the names of the tribes of their fathers.

verses.kjv: According to the lot shall the possession thereof be divided between many and few.
verses.bsb: Each inheritance is to be divided by lot among the larger and smaller tribes.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And these are they that were numbered of the Levites after their families: of Gershon, the family of the Gershonites: of Kohath, the family of the Kohathites: of Merari, the family of the Merarites.
verses.bsb: Now these were the Levites numbered by their clans:  The Gershonite clan from Gershon,   the Kohathite clan from Kohath,   and the Merarite clan from Merari.

verses.kjv: These are the families of the Levites: the family of the Libnites, the family of the Hebronites, the family of the Mahlites, the family of the Mushites, the family of the Korathites. And Kohath begat Amram.
verses.bsb: These were the families of the Levites:  The Libnite clan,   the Hebronite clan,   the Mahlite clan,   the Mushite clan,   and the Korahite clan.   Now Kohath was the father of Amram,

verses.kjv: And the name of Amram’s wife was Jochebed, the daughter of Levi, whom her mother bare to Levi in Egypt: and she bare unto Amram Aaron and Moses, and Miriam their sister.
verses.bsb: and Amram’s wife was named Jochebed. She was also a daughter of Levi, born to Levi in Egypt. To Amram she bore Aaron, Moses, and their sister Miriam.

verses.kjv: And unto Aaron was born Nadab, and Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar.
verses.bsb: Nadab, Abihu, Eleazar, and Ithamar were born to Aaron,

verses.kjv: And Nadab and Abihu died, when they offered strange fire before the Lord.
verses.bsb: but Nadab and Abihu died when they offered unauthorized fire before the LORD.

verses.kjv: And those that were numbered of them were twenty and three thousand, all males from a month old and upward: for they were not numbered among the children of Israel, because there was no inheritance given them among the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: The registration of the Levites totaled 23,000, every male a month old or more; they were not numbered among the other Israelites, because no inheritance was given to them among the Israelites.

verses.kjv: ¶ These are they that were numbered by Moses and Eleazar the priest, who numbered the children of Israel in the plains of Moab by Jordan near Jericho.
verses.bsb: These were the ones numbered by Moses and Eleazar the priest when they counted the Israelites on the plains of Moab by the Jordan, across from Jericho.

verses.kjv: But among these there was not a man of them whom Moses and Aaron the priest numbered, when they numbered the children of Israel in the wilderness of Sinai.
verses.bsb: Among all these, however, there was not one who had been numbered by Moses and Aaron the priest when they counted the Israelites in the Wilderness of Sinai.

verses.kjv: For the Lord had said of them, They shall surely die in the wilderness. And there was not left a man of them, save Caleb the son of Jephunneh, and Joshua the son of Nun.
verses.bsb: For the LORD had told them that they would surely die in the wilderness. Not one was left except Caleb son of Jephunneh and Joshua son of Nun.

verses.kjv: THEN came the daughters of Zelophehad, the son of Hepher, the son of Gilead, the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh, of the families of Manasseh the son of Joseph: and these are the names of his daughters; Mahlah, Noah, and Hoglah, and Milcah, and Tirzah.
verses.bsb: Now the daughters of Zelophehad son of Hepher, the son of Gilead, the son of Machir, the son of Manasseh, belonged to the clans of Manasseh son of Joseph. These were the names of his daughters: Mahlah, Noah, Hoglah, Milcah, and Tirzah. They approached

verses.kjv: And they stood before Moses, and before Eleazar the priest, and before the princes and all the congregation, by the door of the tabernacle of the congregation, saying,
verses.bsb: the entrance to the Tent of Meeting, stood before Moses, Eleazar the priest, the leaders, and the whole congregation, and said,

verses.kjv: Our father died in the wilderness, and he was not in the company of them that gathered themselves together against the Lord in the company of Korah; but died in his own sin, and had no sons.
verses.bsb: “Our father died in the wilderness, but he was not among the followers of Korah who gathered together against the LORD. Instead, he died because of his own sin, and he had no sons.

verses.kjv: Why should the name of our father be done away from among his family, because he hath no son? Give unto us therefore a possession among the brethren of our father.
verses.bsb: Why should the name of our father disappear from his clan because he had no sons? Give us property among our father’s brothers.”

verses.kjv: And Moses brought their cause before the Lord.
verses.bsb: So Moses brought their case before the LORD,

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: and the LORD answered him,

verses.kjv: The daughters of Zelophehad speak right: thou shalt surely give them a possession of an inheritance among their father’s brethren; and thou shalt cause the inheritance of their father to pass unto them.
verses.bsb: “The daughters of Zelophehad speak correctly. You certainly must give them property as an inheritance among their father’s brothers, and transfer their father’s inheritance to them.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt speak unto the children of Israel, saying, If a man die, and have no son, then ye shall cause his inheritance to pass unto his daughter.
verses.bsb: Furthermore, you shall say to the Israelites, ‘If a man dies and leaves no son, you are to transfer his inheritance to his daughter.

verses.kjv: And if he have no daughter, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his brethren.
verses.bsb: If he has no daughter, give his inheritance to his brothers.

verses.kjv: And if he have no brethren, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his father’s brethren.
verses.bsb: If he has no brothers, give his inheritance to his father’s brothers.

verses.kjv: And if his father have no brethren, then ye shall give his inheritance unto his kinsman that is next to him of his family, and he shall possess it: and it shall be unto the children of Israel a statute of judgment, as the Lord commanded Moses.
verses.bsb: And if his father has no brothers, give his inheritance to the next of kin from his clan, that he may take possession of it. This is to be a statutory ordinance for the Israelites, as the LORD has commanded Moses.’”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Get thee up into this mount Abarim, and see the land which I have given unto the children of Israel.
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses, “Go up this mountain of the Abarim range and see the land that I have given the Israelites.

verses.kjv: And when thou hast seen it, thou also shalt be gathered unto thy people, as Aaron thy brother was gathered.
verses.bsb: After you have seen it, you too will be gathered to your people, as your brother Aaron was;

verses.kjv: For ye rebelled against my commandment in the desert of Zin, in the strife of the congregation, to sanctify me at the water before their eyes: that is the water of Meribah in Kadesh in the wilderness of Zin.
verses.bsb: for when the congregation contended in the Wilderness of Zin, both of you rebelled against My command to show My holiness in their sight regarding the waters.” Those were the waters of Meribah in Kadesh, in the Wilderness of Zin.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses spake unto the Lord, saying,
verses.bsb: So Moses appealed to the LORD,

verses.kjv: Let the Lord, the God of the spirits of all flesh, set a man over the congregation,
verses.bsb: “May the LORD, the God of the spirits of all flesh, appoint a man over the congregation

verses.kjv: Which may go out before them, and which may go in before them, and which may lead them out, and which may bring them in; that the congregation of the Lord be not as sheep which have no shepherd.
verses.bsb: who will go out and come in before them, and who will lead them out and bring them in, so that the congregation of the LORD will not be like sheep without a shepherd.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord said unto Moses, Take thee Joshua the son of Nun, a man in whom is the spirit, and lay thine hand upon him;
verses.bsb: And the LORD replied to Moses, “Take Joshua son of Nun, a man with the Spirit in him, and lay your hands on him.

verses.kjv: And set him before Eleazar the priest, and before all the congregation; and give him a charge in their sight.
verses.bsb: Have him stand before Eleazar the priest and the whole congregation, and commission him in their sight.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt put some of thine honour upon him, that all the congregation of the children of Israel may be obedient.
verses.bsb: Confer on him some of your authority, so that the whole congregation of Israel will obey him.

verses.kjv: And he shall stand before Eleazar the priest, who shall ask counsel for him after the judgment of Urim before the Lord: at his word shall they go out, and at his word they shall come in, both he, and all the children of Israel with him, even all the congregation.
verses.bsb: He shall stand before Eleazar the priest, who will seek counsel for him before the LORD by the judgment of the Urim. At his command, he and all the Israelites with him—the entire congregation—will go out and come in.”

verses.kjv: And Moses did as the Lord commanded him: and he took Joshua, and set him before Eleazar the priest, and before all the congregation:
verses.bsb: Moses did as the LORD had commanded him. He took Joshua, had him stand before Eleazar the priest and the whole congregation,

verses.kjv: And he laid his hands upon him, and gave him a charge, as the Lord commanded by the hand of Moses.
verses.bsb: and laid his hands on him and commissioned him, as the LORD had instructed through Moses.

verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: Then the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: Command the children of Israel, and say unto them, My offering, and my bread for my sacrifices made by fire, for a sweet savour unto me, shall ye observe to offer unto me in their due season.
verses.bsb: “Command the Israelites and say to them: See that you present to Me at its appointed time the food for My offerings by fire, as a pleasing aroma to Me.

verses.kjv: And thou shalt say unto them, This is the offering made by fire which ye shall offer unto the Lord; two lambs of the first year without spot day by day, for a continual burnt offering.
verses.bsb: And tell them that this is the offering made by fire you are to present to the LORD as a regular burnt offering each day: two unblemished year-old male lambs.

verses.kjv: The one lamb shalt thou offer in the morning, and the other lamb shalt thou offer at even;
verses.bsb: Offer one lamb in the morning and the other at twilight,

verses.kjv: And a tenth part of an ephah of flour for a meat offering, mingled with the fourth part of an hin of beaten oil.
verses.bsb: along with a tenth of an ephah of fine flour as a grain offering, mixed with a quarter hin of oil from pressed olives.

verses.kjv: It is a continual burnt offering, which was ordained in mount Sinai for a sweet savour, a sacrifice made by fire unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: This is a regular burnt offering established at Mount Sinai as a pleasing aroma, an offering made by fire to the LORD.

verses.kjv: And the drink offering thereof shall be the fourth part of an hin for the one lamb: in the holy place shalt thou cause the strong wine to be poured unto the Lord for a drink offering.
verses.bsb: The drink offering accompanying each lamb shall be a quarter hin. Pour out the offering of fermented drink to the LORD in the sanctuary area.

verses.kjv: And the other lamb shalt thou offer at even: as the meat offering of the morning, and as the drink offering thereof, thou shalt offer it, a sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: And offer the second lamb at twilight, with the same grain offering and drink offering as in the morning. It is an offering made by fire, a pleasing aroma to the LORD.

verses.kjv: ¶ And on the sabbath day two lambs of the first year without spot, and two tenth deals of flour for a meat offering, mingled with oil, and the drink offering thereof:
verses.bsb: On the Sabbath day, present two unblemished year-old male lambs, accompanied by a grain offering of two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil, as well as a drink offering.

verses.kjv: This is the burnt offering of every sabbath, beside the continual burnt offering, and his drink offering.
verses.bsb: This is the burnt offering for every Sabbath, in addition to the regular burnt offering and its drink offering.

verses.kjv: ¶ And in the beginnings of your months ye shall offer a burnt offering unto the Lord; two young bullocks, and one ram, seven lambs of the first year without spot;
verses.bsb: At the beginning of every month, you are to present to the LORD a burnt offering of two young bulls, one ram, and seven male lambs a year old, all unblemished,

verses.kjv: And three tenth deals of flour for a meat offering, mingled with oil, for one bullock; and two tenth deals of flour for a meat offering, mingled with oil, for one ram;
verses.bsb: along with three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering with each bull, two-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering with the ram,

verses.kjv: And a several tenth deal of flour mingled with oil for a meat offering unto one lamb; for a burnt offering of a sweet savour, a sacrifice made by fire unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: and a tenth of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil as a grain offering with each lamb. This is a burnt offering, a pleasing aroma, an offering made by fire to the LORD.

verses.kjv: And their drink offerings shall be half an hin of wine unto a bullock, and the third part of an hin unto a ram, and a fourth part of an hin unto a lamb: this is the burnt offering of every month throughout the months of the year.
verses.bsb: Their drink offerings shall be half a hin of wine with each bull, a third of a hin with the ram, and a quarter hin with each lamb. This is the monthly burnt offering to be made at each new moon throughout the year.

verses.kjv: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering unto the Lord shall be offered, beside the continual burnt offering, and his drink offering.
verses.bsb: In addition to the regular burnt offering with its drink offering, one male goat is to be presented to the LORD as a sin offering.

verses.kjv: And in the fourteenth day of the first month is the passover of the Lord.
verses.bsb: The fourteenth day of the first month is the LORD’s Passover.

verses.kjv: And in the fifteenth day of this month is the feast: seven days shall unleavened bread be eaten.
verses.bsb: On the fifteenth day of this month, there shall be a feast; for seven days unleavened bread is to be eaten.

verses.kjv: In the first day shall be an holy convocation; ye shall do no manner of servile work therein:
verses.bsb: On the first day there is to be a sacred assembly; you must not do any regular work.

verses.kjv: But ye shall offer a sacrifice made by fire for a burnt offering unto the Lord; two young bullocks, and one ram, and seven lambs of the first year: they shall be unto you without blemish:
verses.bsb: Present to the LORD an offering made by fire, a burnt offering of two young bulls, one ram, and seven male lambs a year old, all unblemished.

verses.kjv: And their meat offering shall be of flour mingled with oil: three tenth deals shall ye offer for a bullock, and two tenth deals for a ram;
verses.bsb: The grain offering shall consist of fine flour mixed with oil; offer three-tenths of an ephah with each bull, two-tenths of an ephah with the ram,

verses.kjv: A several tenth deal shalt thou offer for every lamb, throughout the seven lambs:
verses.bsb: and a tenth of an ephah with each of the seven lambs.

verses.kjv: And one goat for a sin offering, to make an atonement for you.
verses.bsb: Include one male goat as a sin offering to make atonement for you.

verses.kjv: Ye shall offer these beside the burnt offering in the morning, which is for a continual burnt offering.
verses.bsb: You are to present these in addition to the regular morning burnt offering.

verses.kjv: After this manner ye shall offer daily, throughout the seven days, the meat of the sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord: it shall be offered beside the continual burnt offering, and his drink offering.
verses.bsb: Offer the same food each day for seven days as an offering made by fire, a pleasing aroma to the LORD. It is to be offered with its drink offering and the regular burnt offering.

verses.kjv: And on the seventh day ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work.
verses.bsb: On the seventh day you shall hold a sacred assembly; you must not do any regular work.

verses.kjv: ¶ Also in the day of the firstfruits, when ye bring a new meat offering unto the Lord, after your weeks be out, ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work:
verses.bsb: On the day of firstfruits, when you present an offering of new grain to the LORD during the Feast of Weeks, you are to hold a sacred assembly; you must not do any regular work.

verses.kjv: But ye shall offer the burnt offering for a sweet savour unto the Lord; two young bullocks, one ram, seven lambs of the first year;
verses.bsb: Present a burnt offering of two young bulls, one ram, and seven male lambs a year old as a pleasing aroma to the LORD,

verses.kjv: And their meat offering of flour mingled with oil, three tenth deals unto one bullock, two tenth deals unto one ram,
verses.bsb: together with their grain offerings of fine flour mixed with oil—three-tenths of an ephah with each bull, two-tenths of an ephah with the ram,

verses.kjv: A several tenth deal unto one lamb, throughout the seven lambs;
verses.bsb: and a tenth of an ephah with each of the seven lambs.

verses.kjv: And one kid of the goats, to make an atonement for you.
verses.bsb: Include one male goat to make atonement for you.

verses.kjv: Ye shall offer them beside the continual burnt offering, and his meat offering, (they shall be unto you without blemish) and their drink offerings.
verses.bsb: Offer them with their drink offerings in addition to the regular burnt offering and its grain offering. The animals must be unblemished.

verses.kjv: AND in the seventh month, on the first day of the month, ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work: it is a day of blowing the trumpets unto you.
verses.bsb: “On the first day of the seventh month, you are to hold a sacred assembly, and you must not do any regular work. This will be a day for you to sound the trumpets.

verses.kjv: And ye shall offer a burnt offering for a sweet savour unto the Lord; one young bullock, one ram, and seven lambs of the first year without blemish:
verses.bsb: As a pleasing aroma to the LORD, you are to present a burnt offering of one young bull, one ram, and seven male lambs a year old, all unblemished,

verses.kjv: And their meat offering shall be of flour mingled with oil, three tenth deals for a bullock, and two tenth deals for a ram,
verses.bsb: together with their grain offerings of fine flour mixed with oil—three-tenths of an ephah with the bull, two-tenths of an ephah with the ram,

verses.kjv: And one tenth deal for one lamb, throughout the seven lambs:
verses.bsb: and a tenth of an ephah with each of the seven male lambs.

verses.kjv: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering, to make an atonement for you:
verses.bsb: Include one male goat as a sin offering to make atonement for you.

verses.kjv: Beside the burnt offering of the month, and his meat offering, and the daily burnt offering, and his meat offering, and their drink offerings, according unto their manner, for a sweet savour, a sacrifice made by fire unto the Lord.
verses.bsb: These are in addition to the monthly and daily burnt offerings with their prescribed grain offerings and drink offerings. They are a pleasing aroma, an offering made by fire to the LORD.

verses.kjv: ¶ And ye shall have on the tenth day of this seventh month an holy convocation; and ye shall afflict your souls: ye shall not do any work therein:
verses.bsb: On the tenth day of this seventh month, you are to hold a sacred assembly, and you shall humble yourselves; you must not do any work.

verses.kjv: But ye shall offer a burnt offering unto the Lord for a sweet savour; one young bullock, one ram, and seven lambs of the first year; they shall be unto you without blemish:
verses.bsb: Present as a pleasing aroma to the LORD a burnt offering of one young bull, one ram, and seven male lambs a year old, all unblemished,

verses.kjv: And their meat offering shall be of flour mingled with oil, three tenth deals to a bullock, and two tenth deals to one ram,
verses.bsb: together with their grain offerings of fine flour mixed with oil—three-tenths of an ephah with the bull, two-tenths of an ephah with the ram,

verses.kjv: A several tenth deal for one lamb, throughout the seven lambs:
verses.bsb: and a tenth of an ephah with each of the seven lambs.

verses.kjv: One kid of the goats for a sin offering; beside the sin offering of atonement, and the continual burnt offering, and the meat offering of it, and their drink offerings.
verses.bsb: Include one male goat for a sin offering, in addition to the sin offering of atonement and the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offerings.

verses.kjv: ¶ And on the fifteenth day of the seventh month ye shall have an holy convocation; ye shall do no servile work, and ye shall keep a feast unto the Lord seven days:
verses.bsb: On the fifteenth day of the seventh month, you are to hold a sacred assembly; you must not do any regular work, and you shall observe a feast to the LORD for seven days.

verses.kjv: And ye shall offer a burnt offering, a sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord; thirteen young bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year; they shall be without blemish:
verses.bsb: As a pleasing aroma to the LORD, you are to present an offering made by fire, a burnt offering of thirteen young bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs a year old, all unblemished,

verses.kjv: And their meat offering shall be of flour mingled with oil, three tenth deals unto every bullock of the thirteen bullocks, two tenth deals to each ram of the two rams,
verses.bsb: along with the grain offering of three-tenths of an ephah of fine flour mixed with oil with each of the thirteen bulls, two-tenths of an ephah with each of the two rams,

verses.kjv: And a several tenth deal to each lamb of the fourteen lambs:
verses.bsb: and a tenth of an ephah with each of the fourteen lambs.

verses.kjv: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, his meat offering, and his drink offering.
verses.bsb: Include one male goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.

verses.kjv: ¶ And on the second day ye shall offer twelve young bullocks, two rams, fourteen lambs of the first year without spot:
verses.bsb: On the second day you are to present twelve young bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs a year old, all unblemished,

verses.kjv: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
verses.bsb: along with the grain and drink offerings for the bulls, rams, and lambs, according to the number prescribed.

verses.kjv: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, and the meat offering thereof, and their drink offerings.
verses.bsb: Include one male goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.

verses.kjv: ¶ And on the third day eleven bullocks, two rams, fourteen lambs of the first year without blemish;
verses.bsb: On the third day you are to present eleven bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs a year old, all unblemished,

verses.kjv: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
verses.bsb: along with the grain and drink offerings for the bulls, rams, and lambs, according to the number prescribed.

verses.kjv: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, and his meat offering, and his drink offering.
verses.bsb: Include one male goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.

verses.kjv: ¶ And on the fourth day ten bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year without blemish:
verses.bsb: On the fourth day you are to present ten bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs a year old, all unblemished,

verses.kjv: Their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
verses.bsb: along with the grain and drink offerings for the bulls, rams, and lambs, according to the number prescribed.

verses.kjv: And one kid of the goats for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, his meat offering, and his drink offering.
verses.bsb: Include one male goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.

verses.kjv: ¶ And on the fifth day nine bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year without spot:
verses.bsb: On the fifth day you are to present nine bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs a year old, all unblemished,

verses.kjv: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
verses.bsb: along with the grain and drink offerings for the bulls, rams, and lambs, according to the number prescribed.

verses.kjv: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, and his meat offering, and his drink offering.
verses.bsb: Include one male goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.

verses.kjv: ¶ And on the sixth day eight bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year without blemish:
verses.bsb: On the sixth day you are to present eight bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs a year old, all unblemished,

verses.kjv: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
verses.bsb: along with the grain and drink offerings for the bulls, rams, and lambs, according to the number prescribed.

verses.kjv: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, his meat offering, and his drink offering.
verses.bsb: Include one male goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.

verses.kjv: ¶ And on the seventh day seven bullocks, two rams, and fourteen lambs of the first year without blemish:
verses.bsb: On the seventh day you are to present seven bulls, two rams, and fourteen male lambs a year old, all unblemished,

verses.kjv: And their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullocks, for the rams, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
verses.bsb: along with the grain and drink offerings for the bulls, rams, and lambs, according to the number prescribed.

verses.kjv: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, his meat offering, and his drink offering.
verses.bsb: Include one male goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.

verses.kjv: ¶ On the eighth day ye shall have a solemn assembly: ye shall do no servile work therein:
verses.bsb: On the eighth day you are to hold a solemn assembly; you must not do any regular work.

verses.kjv: But ye shall offer a burnt offering, a sacrifice made by fire, of a sweet savour unto the Lord: one bullock, one ram, seven lambs of the first year without blemish:
verses.bsb: As a pleasing aroma to the LORD, you are to present an offering made by fire, a burnt offering of one bull, one ram, and seven male lambs a year old, all unblemished,

verses.kjv: Their meat offering and their drink offerings for the bullock, for the ram, and for the lambs, shall be according to their number, after the manner:
verses.bsb: along with the grain and drink offerings for the bulls, rams, and lambs, according to the number prescribed.

verses.kjv: And one goat for a sin offering; beside the continual burnt offering, and his meat offering, and his drink offering.
verses.bsb: Include one male goat as a sin offering, in addition to the regular burnt offering with its grain offering and drink offering.

verses.kjv: These things ye shall do unto the Lord in your set feasts, beside your vows, and your freewill offerings, for your burnt offerings, and for your meat offerings, and for your drink offerings, and for your peace offerings.
verses.bsb: You are to present these offerings to the LORD at your appointed times, in addition to your vow and freewill offerings, whether burnt offerings, grain offerings, drink offerings, or peace offerings.”

verses.kjv: And Moses told the children of Israel according to all that the Lord commanded Moses.
verses.bsb: So Moses spoke all this to the Israelites just as the LORD had commanded him.

verses.kjv: AND Moses spake unto the heads of the tribes concerning the children of Israel, saying, This is the thing which the Lord hath commanded.
verses.bsb: Then Moses said to the heads of the tribes of Israel, “This is what the LORD has commanded:

verses.kjv: If a man vow a vow unto the Lord, or swear an oath to bind his soul with a bond; he shall not break his word, he shall do according to all that proceedeth out of his mouth.
verses.bsb: If a man makes a vow to the LORD or swears an oath to obligate himself by a pledge, he must not break his word; he must do everything he has promised.

verses.kjv: If a woman also vow a vow unto the Lord, and bind herself by a bond, being in her father’s house in her youth;
verses.bsb: And if a woman in her father’s house during her youth makes a vow to the LORD or obligates herself by a pledge,

verses.kjv: And her father hear her vow, and her bond wherewith she hath bound her soul, and her father shall hold his peace at her: then all her vows shall stand, and every bond wherewith she hath bound her soul shall stand.
verses.bsb: and her father hears about her vow or pledge but says nothing to her, then all the vows or pledges by which she has bound herself shall stand.

verses.kjv: But if her father disallow her in the day that he heareth; not any of her vows, or of her bonds wherewith she hath bound her soul, shall stand: and the Lord shall forgive her, because her father disallowed her.
verses.bsb: But if her father prohibits her on the day he hears about it, then none of the vows or pledges by which she has bound herself shall stand. The LORD will absolve her because her father has prohibited her.

verses.kjv: And if she had at all an husband, when she vowed, or uttered ought out of her lips, wherewith she bound her soul;
verses.bsb: If a woman marries while under a vow or rash promise by which she has bound herself,

verses.kjv: And her husband heard it, and held his peace at her in the day that he heard it: then her vows shall stand, and her bonds wherewith she bound her soul shall stand.
verses.bsb: and her husband hears of it but says nothing to her on that day, then the vows or pledges by which she has bound herself shall stand.

verses.kjv: But if her husband disallowed her on the day that he heard it; then he shall make her vow which she vowed, and that which she uttered with her lips, wherewith she bound her soul, of none effect: and the Lord shall forgive her.
verses.bsb: But if her husband prohibits her when he hears of it, he nullifies the vow that binds her or the rash promise she has made, and the LORD will absolve her.

verses.kjv: But every vow of a widow, and of her that is divorced, wherewith they have bound their souls, shall stand against her.
verses.bsb: Every vow a widow or divorced woman pledges to fulfill is binding on her.

verses.kjv: And if she vowed in her husband’s house, or bound her soul by a bond with an oath;
verses.bsb: If a woman in her husband’s house has made a vow or put herself under an obligation with an oath,

verses.kjv: And her husband heard it, and held his peace at her, and disallowed her not: then all her vows shall stand, and every bond wherewith she bound her soul shall stand.
verses.bsb: and her husband hears of it but says nothing to her and does not prohibit her, then all the vows or pledges by which she has bound herself shall stand.

verses.kjv: But if her husband hath utterly made them void on the day he heard them; then whatsoever proceeded out of her lips concerning her vows, or concerning the bond of her soul, shall not stand: her husband hath made them void; and the Lord shall forgive her.
verses.bsb: But if her husband nullifies them on the day he hears of them, then nothing that came from her lips, whether her vows or pledges, shall stand. Her husband has nullified them, and the LORD will absolve her.

verses.kjv: Every vow, and every binding oath to afflict the soul, her husband may establish it, or her husband may make it void.
verses.bsb: Her husband may confirm or nullify any vow or any sworn pledge to deny herself.

verses.kjv: But if her husband altogether hold his peace at her from day to day; then he establisheth all her vows, or all her bonds, which are upon her: he confirmeth them, because he held his peace at her in the day that he heard them.
verses.bsb: But if her husband says nothing to her from day to day, then he confirms all the vows and pledges that bind her. He has confirmed them, because he said nothing to her on the day he heard about them.

verses.kjv: But if he shall any ways make them void after that he hath heard them; then he shall bear her iniquity.
verses.bsb: But if he nullifies them after he hears of them, then he will bear her iniquity.”

verses.kjv: These are the statutes, which the Lord commanded Moses, between a man and his wife, between the father and his daughter, being yet in her youth in her father’s house.
verses.bsb: These are the statutes that the LORD commanded Moses concerning the relationship between a man and his wife, and between a father and a young daughter still in his home.

verses.kjv: AND the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: And the LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: Avenge the children of Israel of the Midianites: afterward shalt thou be gathered unto thy people.
verses.bsb: “Take vengeance on the Midianites for the Israelites. After that, you will be gathered to your people.”

verses.kjv: And Moses spake unto the people, saying, Arm some of yourselves unto the war, and let them go against the Midianites, and avenge the Lord of Midian.
verses.bsb: So Moses told the people, “Arm some of your men for war, that they may go against the Midianites and execute the LORD’s vengeance on them.

verses.kjv: Of every tribe a thousand, throughout all the tribes of Israel, shall ye send to the war.
verses.bsb: Send into battle a thousand men from each tribe of Israel.”

verses.kjv: So there were delivered out of the thousands of Israel, a thousand of every tribe, twelve thousand armed for war.
verses.bsb: So a thousand men were recruited from each tribe of Israel—twelve thousand armed for war.

verses.kjv: And Moses sent them to the war, a thousand of every tribe, them and Phinehas the son of Eleazar the priest, to the war, with the holy instruments, and the trumpets to blow in his hand.
verses.bsb: And Moses sent the thousand from each tribe into battle, along with Phinehas son of Eleazar the priest, who took with him the vessels of the sanctuary and the trumpets for signaling.

verses.kjv: And they warred against the Midianites, as the Lord commanded Moses; and they slew all the males.
verses.bsb: Then they waged war against Midian, as the LORD had commanded Moses, and they killed every male.

verses.kjv: And they slew the kings of Midian, beside the rest of them that were slain; namely, Evi, and Rekem, and Zur, and Hur, and Reba, five kings of Midian: Balaam also the son of Beor they slew with the sword.
verses.bsb: Among the slain were Evi, Rekem, Zur, Hur, and Reba—the five kings of Midian. They also killed Balaam son of Beor with the sword.

verses.kjv: And the children of Israel took all the women of Midian captives, and their little ones, and took the spoil of all their cattle, and all their flocks, and all their goods.
verses.bsb: The Israelites captured the Midianite women and their children, and they plundered all their herds, flocks, and goods.

verses.kjv: And they burnt all their cities wherein they dwelt, and all their goodly castles, with fire.
verses.bsb: Then they burned all the cities where the Midianites had lived, as well as all their encampments,

verses.kjv: And they took all the spoil, and all the prey, both of men and of beasts.
verses.bsb: and carried away all the plunder and spoils, both people and animals.

verses.kjv: And they brought the captives, and the prey, and the spoil, unto Moses, and Eleazar the priest, and unto the congregation of the children of Israel, unto the camp at the plains of Moab, which are by Jordan near Jericho.
verses.bsb: They brought the captives, spoils, and plunder to Moses, to Eleazar the priest, and to the congregation of Israel at the camp on the plains of Moab, by the Jordan across from Jericho.

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses, and Eleazar the priest, and all the princes of the congregation, went forth to meet them without the camp.
verses.bsb: And Moses, Eleazar the priest, and all the leaders of the congregation went to meet them outside the camp.

verses.kjv: And Moses was wroth with the officers of the host, with the captains over thousands, and captains over hundreds, which came from the battle.
verses.bsb: But Moses was angry with the officers of the army—the commanders of thousands and commanders of hundreds—who were returning from the battle.

verses.kjv: And Moses said unto them, Have ye saved all the women alive?
verses.bsb: “Have you spared all the women?” he asked them.

verses.kjv: Behold, these caused the children of Israel, through the counsel of Balaam, to commit trespass against the Lord in the matter of Peor, and there was a plague among the congregation of the Lord.
verses.bsb: “Look, these women caused the sons of Israel, through the counsel of Balaam, to turn unfaithfully against the LORD at Peor, so that the plague struck the congregation of the LORD.

verses.kjv: Now therefore kill every male among the little ones, and kill every woman that hath known man by lying with him.
verses.bsb: So now, kill all the boys, as well as every woman who has had relations with a man,

verses.kjv: But all the women children, that have not known a man by lying with him, keep alive for yourselves.
verses.bsb: but spare for yourselves every girl who has never had relations with a man.

verses.kjv: And do ye abide without the camp seven days: whosoever hath killed any person, and whosoever hath touched any slain, purify both yourselves and your captives on the third day, and on the seventh day.
verses.bsb: All of you who have killed a person or touched the dead are to remain outside the camp for seven days. On the third day and the seventh day you are to purify both yourselves and your captives.

verses.kjv: And purify all your raiment, and all that is made of skins, and all work of goats’ hair, and all things made of wood.
verses.bsb: And purify every garment and leather good, everything made of goat’s hair, and every article of wood.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Eleazar the priest said unto the men of war which went to the battle, This is the ordinance of the law which the Lord commanded Moses;
verses.bsb: Then Eleazar the priest said to the soldiers who had gone into battle, “This is the statute of the law which the LORD has commanded Moses:

verses.kjv: Only the gold, and the silver, the brass, the iron, the tin, and the lead,
verses.bsb: Only the gold, silver, bronze, iron, tin, and lead—

verses.kjv: Every thing that may abide the fire, ye shall make it go through the fire, and it shall be clean: nevertheless it shall be purified with the water of separation: and all that abideth not the fire ye shall make go through the water.
verses.bsb: everything that can withstand the fire—must be put through the fire, and it will be clean. But it must still be purified with the water of purification. And everything that cannot withstand the fire must pass through the water.

verses.kjv: And ye shall wash your clothes on the seventh day, and ye shall be clean, and afterward ye shall come into the camp.
verses.bsb: On the seventh day you are to wash your clothes, and you will be clean. After that you may enter the camp.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And the Lord spake unto Moses, saying,
verses.bsb: The LORD said to Moses,

verses.kjv: Take the sum of the prey that was taken, both of man and of beast, thou, and Eleazar the priest, and the chief fathers of the congregation:
verses.bsb: “You and Eleazar the priest and the family heads of the congregation are to take a count of what was captured, both of man and beast.

verses.kjv: And divide the prey into two parts; between them that took the war upon them, who went out to battle, and between all the congregation:
verses.bsb: Then divide the captives between the troops who went out to battle and the rest of the congregation.

verses.kjv: And levy a tribute unto the Lord of the men of war which went out to battle: one soul of five hundred, both of the persons, and of the beeves, and of the asses, and of the sheep:
verses.bsb: Set aside a tribute for the LORD from what belongs to the soldiers who went into battle: one out of every five hundred, whether persons, cattle, donkeys, or sheep.

verses.kjv: Take it of their half, and give it unto Eleazar the priest, for an heave offering of the Lord.
verses.bsb: Take it from their half and give it to Eleazar the priest as an offering to the LORD.

verses.kjv: And of the children of Israel’s half, thou shalt take one portion of fifty, of the persons, of the beeves, of the asses, and of the flocks, of all manner of beasts, and give them unto the Levites, which keep the charge of the tabernacle of the Lord.
verses.bsb: From the Israelites’ half, take one out of every fifty, whether persons, cattle, donkeys, sheep, or other animals, and give them to the Levites who keep charge of the tabernacle of the LORD.”

verses.kjv: And Moses and Eleazar the priest did as the Lord commanded Moses.
verses.bsb: So Moses and Eleazar the priest did as the LORD had commanded Moses,

verses.kjv: And the booty, being the rest of the prey which the men of war had caught, was six hundred thousand and seventy thousand and five thousand sheep,
verses.bsb: and this plunder remained from the spoils the soldiers had taken:  675,000 sheep,

verses.kjv: And threescore and twelve thousand beeves,
verses.bsb: 72,000 cattle,

verses.kjv: And threescore and one thousand asses,
verses.bsb: 61,000 donkeys,

verses.kjv: And thirty and two thousand persons in all, of women that had not known man by lying with him.
verses.bsb: and 32,000 women who had not slept with a man.

verses.kjv: And the half, which was the portion of them that went out to war, was in number three hundred thousand and seven and thirty thousand and five hundred sheep:
verses.bsb: This was the half portion for those who had gone to war:

verses.kjv: And the Lord’s tribute of the sheep was six hundred and threescore and fifteen.
verses.bsb: 337,500 sheep, including a tribute to the LORD of 675,

verses.kjv: And the beeves were thirty and six thousand; of which the Lord’s tribute was threescore and twelve.
verses.bsb: 36,000 cattle, including a tribute to the LORD of 72,

verses.kjv: And the asses were thirty thousand and five hundred; of which the Lord’s tribute was threescore and one.
verses.bsb: 30,500 donkeys, including a tribute to the LORD of 61,

verses.kjv: And the persons were sixteen thousand; of which the Lord’s tribute was thirty and two persons.
verses.bsb: and 16,000 people, including a tribute to the LORD of 32.

verses.kjv: And Moses gave the tribute, which was the Lord’s heave offering, unto Eleazar the priest, as the Lord commanded Moses.
verses.bsb: Moses gave the tribute to Eleazar the priest as an offering for the LORD, as the LORD had commanded Moses.

verses.kjv: And of the children of Israel’s half, which Moses divided from the men that warred,
verses.bsb: From the Israelites’ half, which Moses had set apart from the men who had gone to war,

verses.kjv: (Now the half that pertained unto the congregation was three hundred thousand and thirty thousand and seven thousand and five hundred sheep,
verses.bsb: this half belonged to the congregation:  337,500 sheep,

verses.kjv: And thirty and six thousand beeves,
verses.bsb: 36,000 cattle,

verses.kjv: And thirty thousand asses and five hundred,
verses.bsb: 30,500 donkeys,

verses.kjv: And sixteen thousand persons;)
verses.bsb: and 16,000 people.

verses.kjv: Even of the children of Israel’s half, Moses took one portion of fifty, both of man and of beast, and gave them unto the Levites, which kept the charge of the tabernacle of the Lord; as the Lord commanded Moses.
verses.bsb: From the Israelites’ half, Moses took one out of every fifty persons and animals and gave them to the Levites who kept charge of the tabernacle of the LORD, as the LORD had commanded him.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the officers which were over thousands of the host, the captains of thousands, and captains of hundreds, came near unto Moses:
verses.bsb: Then the officers who were over the units of the army—the commanders of thousands and of hundreds—approached Moses

verses.kjv: And they said unto Moses, Thy servants have taken the sum of the men of war which are under our charge, and there lacketh not one man of us.
verses.bsb: and said, “Your servants have counted the soldiers under our command, and not one of us is missing.

verses.kjv: We have therefore brought an oblation for the Lord, what every man hath gotten, of jewels of gold, chains, and bracelets, rings, earrings, and tablets, to make an atonement for our souls before the Lord.
verses.bsb: So we have brought to the LORD an offering of the gold articles each man acquired—armlets, bracelets, rings, earrings, and necklaces—to make atonement for ourselves before the LORD.”

verses.kjv: And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold of them, even all wrought jewels.
verses.bsb: So Moses and Eleazar the priest received from them all the articles made out of gold.

verses.kjv: And all the gold of the offering that they offered up to the Lord, of the captains of thousands, and of the captains of hundreds, was sixteen thousand seven hundred and fifty shekels.
verses.bsb: All the gold that the commanders of thousands and of hundreds presented as an offering to the LORD weighed 16,750 shekels.

verses.kjv: (For the men of war had taken spoil, every man for himself.)
verses.bsb: Each of the soldiers had taken plunder for himself.

verses.kjv: And Moses and Eleazar the priest took the gold of the captains of thousands and of hundreds, and brought it into the tabernacle of the congregation, for a memorial for the children of Israel before the Lord.
verses.bsb: And Moses and Eleazar the priest received the gold from the commanders of thousands and of hundreds and brought it into the Tent of Meeting as a memorial for the Israelites before the LORD.

verses.kjv: NOW the children of Reuben and the children of Gad had a very great multitude of cattle: and when they saw the land of Jazer, and the land of Gilead, that, behold, the place was a place for cattle;
verses.bsb: Now the Reubenites and Gadites, who had very large herds and flocks, surveyed the lands of Jazer and Gilead, and they saw that the region was suitable for livestock.

verses.kjv: The children of Gad and the children of Reuben came and spake unto Moses, and to Eleazar the priest, and unto the princes of the congregation, saying,
verses.bsb: So the Gadites and Reubenites came to Moses, Eleazar the priest, and the leaders of the congregation, and said,

verses.kjv: Ataroth, and Dibon, and Jazer, and Nimrah, and Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Shebam, and Nebo, and Beon,
verses.bsb: “Ataroth, Dibon, Jazer, Nimrah, Heshbon, Elealeh, Sebam, Nebo, and Beon,

verses.kjv: Even the country which the Lord smote before the congregation of Israel, is a land for cattle, and thy servants have cattle:
verses.bsb: which the LORD conquered before the congregation of Israel, are suitable for livestock—and your servants have livestock.”

verses.kjv: Wherefore, said they, if we have found grace in thy sight, let this land be given unto thy servants for a possession, and bring us not over Jordan.
verses.bsb: “If we have found favor in your sight,” they said, “let this land be given to your servants as a possession. Do not make us cross the Jordan.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses said unto the children of Gad and to the children of Reuben, Shall your brethren go to war, and shall ye sit here?
verses.bsb: But Moses asked the Gadites and Reubenites, “Shall your brothers go to war while you sit here?

verses.kjv: And wherefore discourage ye the heart of the children of Israel from going over into the land which the Lord hath given them?
verses.bsb: Why are you discouraging the Israelites from crossing into the land that the LORD has given them?

verses.kjv: Thus did your fathers, when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to see the land.
verses.bsb: This is what your fathers did when I sent them from Kadesh-barnea to inspect the land.

verses.kjv: For when they went up unto the valley of Eshcol, and saw the land, they discouraged the heart of the children of Israel, that they should not go into the land which the Lord had given them.
verses.bsb: For when your fathers went up to the Valley of Eshcol and saw the land, they discouraged the Israelites from entering the land that the LORD had given them.

verses.kjv: And the Lord’s anger was kindled the same time, and he sware, saying,
verses.bsb: So the anger of the LORD was kindled that day, and He swore an oath, saying,

verses.kjv: Surely none of the men that came up out of Egypt, from twenty years old and upward, shall see the land which I sware unto Abraham, unto Isaac, and unto Jacob; because they have not wholly followed me:
verses.bsb: ‘Because they did not follow Me wholeheartedly, not one of the men twenty years of age or older who came out of Egypt will see the land that I swore to give Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob—

verses.kjv: Save Caleb the son of Jephunneh the Kenezite, and Joshua the son of Nun: for they have wholly followed the Lord.
verses.bsb: not one except Caleb son of Jephunneh the Kenizzite and Joshua son of Nun—because they did follow the LORD wholeheartedly.’

verses.kjv: And the Lord’s anger was kindled against Israel, and he made them wander in the wilderness forty years, until all the generation, that had done evil in the sight of the Lord, was consumed.
verses.bsb: The anger of the LORD burned against Israel, and He made them wander in the wilderness forty years, until the whole generation who had done evil in His sight was gone.

verses.kjv: And, behold, ye are risen up in your fathers’ stead, an increase of sinful men, to augment yet the fierce anger of the Lord toward Israel.
verses.bsb: Now behold, you, a brood of sinners, have risen up in place of your fathers to further stoke the burning anger of the LORD against Israel.

verses.kjv: For if ye turn away from after him, he will yet again leave them in the wilderness; and ye shall destroy all this people.
verses.bsb: For if you turn away from following Him, He will once again leave this people in the wilderness, and you will be the cause of their destruction.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And they came near unto him, and said, We will build sheepfolds here for our cattle, and cities for our little ones:
verses.bsb: Then the Gadites and Reubenites approached Moses and said, “We want to build sheepfolds here for our livestock and cities for our little ones.

verses.kjv: But we ourselves will go ready armed before the children of Israel, until we have brought them unto their place: and our little ones shall dwell in the fenced cities because of the inhabitants of the land.
verses.bsb: But we will arm ourselves and be ready to go ahead of the Israelites until we have brought them into their place. Meanwhile, our little ones will remain in the fortified cities for protection from the inhabitants of the land.

verses.kjv: We will not return unto our houses, until the children of Israel have inherited every man his inheritance.
verses.bsb: We will not return to our homes until every Israelite has taken possession of his inheritance.

verses.kjv: For we will not inherit with them on yonder side Jordan, or forward; because our inheritance is fallen to us on this side Jordan eastward.
verses.bsb: Yet we will not have an inheritance with them across the Jordan and beyond, because our inheritance has come to us on the east side of the Jordan.”

verses.kjv: ¶ And Moses said unto them, If ye will do this thing, if ye will go armed before the Lord to war,
verses.bsb: Moses replied, “If you will do this—if you will arm yourselves before the LORD for battle,

verses.kjv: And will go all of you armed over Jordan before the Lord, until he hath driven out his enemies from before him,
verses.bsb: and if every one of your armed men crosses the Jordan before the LORD, until He has driven His enemies out before Him,

verses.kjv: And the land be subdued before the Lord: then afterward ye shall return, and be guiltless before the Lord, and before Israel; and this land shall be your possession before the Lord.
verses.bsb: then when the land is subdued before the LORD, you may return and be free of obligation to the LORD and to Israel. And this land will belong to you as a possession before the LORD.

verses.kjv: But if ye will not do so, behold, ye have sinned against the Lord: and be sure your sin will find you out.
verses.bsb: But if you do not do this, you will certainly sin against the LORD—and be assured that your sin will find you out.

verses.kjv: Build you cities for your little ones, and folds for your sheep; and do that which hath proceeded out of your mouth.
verses.bsb: Build cities for your little ones and folds for your flocks, but do what you have promised.”

verses.kjv: And the children of Gad and the children of Reuben spake unto Moses, saying, Thy servants will do as my lord commandeth.
verses.bsb: The Gadites and Reubenites said to Moses, “Your servants will do just as our lord commands.

verses.kjv: Our little ones, our wives, our flocks, and all our cattle, shall be there in the cities of Gilead:
verses.bsb: Our children, our wives, our livestock, and all our animals will remain here in the cities of Gilead.

verses.kjv: But thy servants will pass over, every man armed for war, before the Lord to battle, as my lord saith.
verses.bsb: But your servants are equipped for war, and every man will cross over to the battle before the LORD, just as our lord says.”

verses.kjv: So concerning them Moses commanded Eleazar the priest, and Joshua the son of Nun, and the chief fathers of the tribes of the children of Israel:
verses.bsb: So Moses gave orders about them to Eleazar the priest, to Joshua son of Nun, and to the family leaders of the tribes of Israel.

verses.kjv: And Moses said unto them, If the children of Gad and the children of Reuben will pass with you over Jordan, every man armed to battle, before the Lord, and the land shall be subdued before you; then ye shall give them the land of Gilead for a possession:
verses.bsb: And Moses said to them, “If the Gadites and Reubenites cross the Jordan with you, with every man armed for battle before the LORD, and the land is subdued before you, then you are to give them the land of Gilead as a possession.

verses.kjv: But if they will not pass over with you armed, they shall have possessions among you in the land of Canaan.
verses.bsb: But if they do not arm themselves and go across with you, then they must accept their possession among you in the land of Canaan.”

verses.kjv: And the children of Gad and the children of Reuben answered, saying, As the Lord hath said unto thy servants, so will we do.
verses.bsb: The Gadites and Reubenites replied, “As the LORD has spoken to your servants, so we will do.

verses.kjv: We will pass over armed before the Lord into the land of Canaan, that the possession of our inheritance on this side Jordan may be ours.
verses.bsb: We will cross over into the land of Canaan armed before the LORD, that we may have our inheritance on this side of the Jordan.”

verses.kjv: And Moses gave unto them, even to the children of Gad, and to the children of Reuben, and unto half the tribe of Manasseh the son of Joseph, the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites, and the kingdom of Og king of Bashan, the land, with the cities thereof in the coasts, even the cities of the country round about.
verses.bsb: So Moses gave to the Gadites, to the Reubenites, and to the half-tribe of Manasseh son of Joseph the kingdom of Sihon king of the Amorites and the kingdom of Og king of Bashan—the land including its cities and the territory surrounding them.

verses.kjv: ¶ And the children of Gad built Dibon, and Ataroth, and Aroer,
verses.bsb: And the Gadites built up Dibon, Ataroth, Aroer,

verses.kjv: And Atroth, Shophan, and Jaazer, and Jogbehah,
verses.bsb: Atroth-shophan, Jazer, Jogbehah,

verses.kjv: And Beth-nimrah, and Beth-haran, fenced cities: and folds for sheep.
verses.bsb: Beth-nimrah, and Beth-haran as fortified cities, and they built folds for their flocks.

verses.kjv: And the children of Reuben built Heshbon, and Elealeh, and Kirjathaim,
verses.bsb: The Reubenites built up Heshbon, Elealeh, Kiriathaim,

verses.kjv: And Nebo, and Baal-meon, (their names being changed,) and Shibmah: and gave other names unto the cities which they builded.
verses.bsb: as well as Nebo and Baal-meon (whose names were changed), and Sibmah. And they renamed the cities they rebuilt.

verses.kjv: And the children of Machir the son of Manasseh went to Gilead, and took it, and dispossessed the Amorite which was in it.
verses.bsb: The descendants of Machir son of Manasseh went to Gilead, captured it, and drove out the Amorites who were there.

verses.kjv: And Moses gave Gilead unto Machir the son of Manasseh; and he dwelt therein.
verses.bsb: So Moses gave Gilead to the clan of Machir son of Manasseh, and they settled there.

verses.kjv: And Jair the son of Manasseh went and took the small towns thereof, and called them Havoth-jair.
verses.bsb: Jair, a descendant of Manasseh, went and captured their villages and called them Havvoth-jair.

verses.kjv: And Nobah went and took Kenath, and the villages thereof, and called it Nobah, after his own name.
verses.bsb: And Nobah went and captured Kenath and its villages and called it Nobah, after his own name.

verses.kjv: THESE are the journeys of the children of Israel, which went forth out of the land of Egypt with their armies under the hand of Moses and Aaron.
verses.bsb: These are the journeys of the Israelites when they came out of the land of Egypt by their divisions under the leadership of Moses and Aaron.

verses.kjv: And Moses wrote their goings out according to their journeys by the commandment of the Lord: and these are their journeys according to their goings out.
verses.bsb: At the LORD’s command, Moses recorded the stages of their journey. These are the stages listed by their starting points:

verses.kjv: And they departed from Rameses in the first month, on the fifteenth day of the first month; on the morrow after the passover the children of Israel went out with an high hand in the sight of all the Egyptians.
verses.bsb: On the fifteenth day of the first month, on the day after the Passover, the Israelites set out from Rameses. They marched out defiantly in full view of all the Egyptians,

verses.kjv: For the Egyptians buried all their firstborn, which the Lord had smitten among them: upon their gods also the Lord executed judgments.
verses.bsb: who were burying all their firstborn, whom the LORD had struck down among them; for the LORD had executed judgment against their gods.

verses.kjv: And the children of Israel removed from Rameses, and pitched in Succoth.
verses.bsb: The Israelites set out from Rameses and camped at Succoth.

verses.kjv: And they departed from Succoth, and pitched in Etham, which is in the edge of the wilderness.
verses.bsb: They set out from Succoth and camped at Etham, on the edge of the wilderness.

verses.kjv: And they removed from Etham, and turned again unto Pi-hahiroth, which is before Baal-zephon: and they pitched before Migdol.
verses.bsb: They set out from Etham and turned back to Pi-hahiroth, opposite Baal-zephon, and they camped near Migdol.

verses.kjv: And they departed from before Pi-hahiroth, and passed through the midst of the sea into the wilderness, and went three days’ journey in the wilderness of Etham, and pitched in Marah.
verses.bsb: They set out from Pi-hahiroth and crossed through the sea, into the wilderness, and they journeyed three days into the Wilderness of Etham and camped at Marah.

verses.kjv: And they removed from Marah, and came unto Elim: and in Elim were twelve fountains of water, and threescore and ten palm trees; and they pitched there.
verses.bsb: They set out from Marah and came to Elim, where there were twelve springs and seventy palm trees, and they camped there.

verses.kjv: And they removed from Elim, and encamped by the Red sea.
verses.bsb: They set out from Elim and camped by the Red Sea.

verses.kjv: And they removed from the Red sea, and encamped in the wilderness of Sin.
verses.bsb: They set out from the Red Sea and camped in the Desert of Sin.

verses.kjv: And they took their journey out of the wilderness of Sin, and encamped in Dophkah.
verses.bsb: They set out from the Desert of Sin and camped at Dophkah.

verses.kjv: And they departed from Dophkah, and encamped in Alush.
verses.bsb: They set out from Dophkah and camped at Alush.

verses.kjv: And they removed from Alush, and encamped at Rephidim, where was no water for the people to drink.
verses.bsb: They set out from Alush and camped at Rephidim, where there was no water for the people to drink.

verses.kjv: And they departed from Rephidim, and pitched in the wilderness of Sinai.
verses.bsb: They set out from Rephidim and camped in the Wilderness of Sinai.

verses.kjv: And they removed from the desert of Sinai, and pitched at Kibroth-hattaavah.
verses.bsb: They set out from the Wilderness of Sinai and camped at Kibroth-hattaavah.

verses.kjv: And they departed from Kibroth-hattaavah, and encamped at Hazeroth.
verses.bsb: They set out from Kibroth-hattaavah and camped at Hazeroth.

verses.kjv: And they departed from Hazeroth, and pitched in Rithmah.
verses.bsb: They set out from Hazeroth and camped at Rithmah.

verses.kjv: And they departed from Rithmah, and pitched at Rimmon-parez.
verses.bsb: They set out from Rithmah and camped at Rimmon-perez.

verses.kjv: And they departed from Rimmon-parez, and pitched in Libnah.
verses.bsb: They set out from Rimmon-perez and camped at Libnah.

verses.kjv: And they removed from Libnah, and pitched at Rissah.
verses.bsb: They set out from Libnah and camped at Rissah.

verses.kjv: And they journeyed from Rissah, and pitched in Kehelathah.
verses.bsb: They set out from Rissah and camped at Kehelathah.

verses.kjv: And they went from Kehelathah, and pitched in mount Shapher.
verses.bsb: They set out from Kehelathah and camped at Mount Shepher.

verses.kjv: And they removed from mount Shapher, and encamped in Haradah.
